title: prunus sativa subsp. domestica
intro: Prunus sativa subsp. domestica, also known as wild plum or European plum, is a subspecies of the Prunus sativa plant, which is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark and fruit, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems and skin conditions. The medicinal properties of Prunus sativa subsp. domestica include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and prevent infections. The plant can be prepared as a herbal remedy through infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions and provide various health benefits, including improved digestion and immune function.
